prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the third moon of this year, the guild of SaaS, a mysterious alchemy of numbers and light, hath presented their scrolls of results, much to my befuddlement. They speak of "revenue growth" as if it were a dragon hoarding gold, yet no beast nor treasure is in sight, only ethereal figures dancing upon glowing tablets. The scribes mention "monthly recurring revenue" as though it were a chant to summon rain, yet no clouds gather, only the endless clatter of invisible coins. Verily, they boast of "churn rate" as a measure of loyalty, akin to knights deserting their banners, yet no banners are seen, only the silent departure of shadowy patrons.
